name: ghl-form-css-reference
scope: GoHighLevel / HighLevel / LeadConnector inline form embeds served via form_embed.js. Selectors apply inside the rendered iframe document. Survey forms have additional selectors marked accordingly.
stability_warning: There is no official CSS-class API from HighLevel. Their own help doc punts to DevTools. The DOM is known to change between platform releases — every snapshot is point-in-time. The `data-q` attribute is the single most stable hook because it mirrors the field's internal name set in the form builder; it survives most refactors that break class-name or :nth-child selectors.
how_to_use: Look up a target by `category` + `purpose`. Each entry has a `confidence` field — `confirmed` means at least one community reference has it working in production; `inferred` means it's a reasonable guess that needs DevTools verification on a live form. Prefer `data-q` field targeting over positional selectors.

sources.name: krishnalewin-hash/tourism-ui-kit
sources.notes: Most actively maintained community reference for inline GHL form CSS. Sibling repo: krishnalewin-hash/airport-transfer-form. Battle-tested against the live LeadConnector iframe DOM.

sources.name: Resonant Studios — Coordinator Combo Lead Magnet (live form #HT93KLrv082pr6Wvq9rL)
sources.notes: Empirical findings from styling a live newer-builder GHL form. Confirms that the v1 .ghl-btn.ghl-submit-btn selector did not match this form's button, and that GHL exposes its theme colour via CSS custom properties on :root that must be hijacked to win the cascade.

sources.name: simple10 GHL survey CSS gist
sources.notes: Survey-specific (multi-step). Confirms the `_builder-form` id, the `.ghl-footer` + `.ghl-progress-bar*` family, and the `.hl-app` namespace.

sources.name: Apply Custom CSS to Forms/Surveys/Quizzes
sources.notes: Officially confirms only generic selectors (`input`, `textarea`, `button[type="submit"]`, `label`, `.hl-error`, `.error-message`). Explicitly tells users to use right-click → Inspect for anything specific.

sources.name: HighLevel Ideas — custom CSS classes on form fields
sources.notes: Open feature request for user-assignable classes on form fields. Years-old, never shipped. Confirms there is no per-field class API today.

sources.name: Squarespace forum — GHL form button CSS
sources.notes: Anecdotal colour overrides on submit buttons. No new selectors beyond what's in tourism-ui-kit / simple10.

categories.structure: The form's root element + namespace wrappers.
categories.field-wrapper: Containers that wrap a single field (label + input + error).
categories.field-targeting: How to target a specific field by name. Use `data-q` whenever possible.
categories.field-types: Selectors that vary by field type (checkbox, radio, dropdown, etc.).
categories.labels: Field labels and the required-asterisk markup.
categories.validation: Error states on fields + error message text.
categories.submit: The submit button and its container.
categories.links: Anchor tags inside form copy (terms-and-conditions, privacy links).
categories.survey: Selectors specific to multi-step survey/quiz forms.
categories.host-page: Selectors on the parent page where the iframe is embedded.
categories.theme-variables: GHL's own CSS custom properties — override these at :root to re-skin the form's default theme colour at the source instead of fighting it per-element.

selectors.purpose: Root <form> element inside the iframe document. Use this as the scoping root for all form-level overrides.
Top-level HighLevel app namespace wrapper. Useful for high-specificity overrides like `.hl-app .form-control`.
Container that wraps a single question (label + input + helper text + error).
Bootstrap-style row that contains one or more field wrappers — controls side-by-side field layouts.
Per-field wrapper. Often combined with a Bootstrap col class (e.g. `.col-12.form-field-wrapper` for full-width, `.col-6.form-field-wrapper` for half).
Target a specific field by its internal name. MOST STABLE per-field hook. Replace <field_key> with the field's internal name set in the form builder.
selectors.notes: Prefer this over :nth-child or positional selectors. data-q mirrors the form builder's internal key and survives most DOM refactors.

selectors.purpose: Target the wrapper of a specific field by its internal name. Useful for hiding rows (hidden fields, conditional fields).
selectors.notes: Used in tourism-ui-kit to hide rows containing hidden inputs. Requires :has() — supported in all modern browsers as of 2024.

selectors.purpose: Bootstrap-ish base class on most inputs/selects/textareas. Useful for general typographic + border overrides.
selectors.notes: Also addressable via `.hl-app .form-control` for higher specificity.

selectors.purpose: Vue-powered custom dropdown (used for select fields with rich options).

selectors.selector: input[type="text"], input[type="email"], input[type="tel"], input[type="number"], input[type="url"], input[type="password"], input[type="date"], input[type="search"]
selectors.purpose: Standard text-style HTML inputs. Group target for type/colour/border/radius overrides.
Native checkbox. Use `accent-color` to brand it without rebuilding the control.
Native radio. Use `accent-color` to brand it.
Field label. Sits above the input by default.
Required-field asterisk. Exact markup varies — most common is `<sup>*` appended to the label, but `<span class="required">` is also seen.
selectors.notes: Not confirmed by any community reference. Verify in DevTools when building a new form. The default GHL styling is a red `*` next to the label text.

selectors.purpose: Error state class added to invalid inputs.
Error message text that appears below an invalid field.
selectors.notes: Multiple class names seen across versions — target all four for safety.

selectors.purpose: The form's submit button. `.ghl-btn.ghl-submit-btn` is the official class pair; `button[type="submit"]` is the safe fallback.

selectors.selector: body button, body button[type="submit"], body input[type="submit"], body .ghl-btn, body .ghl-submit-btn, body button[class*="submit"], body button[class*="primary"], body button[class*="btn"], body [role="button"], body form button
selectors.purpose: Broadest-possible submit-button target. Use when the form is rendered by the newer GHL form builder and the standard .ghl-btn.ghl-submit-btn class doesn't match. Empirically required for forms where the v1 selector leaves the button styled in GHL's default theme blue.
selectors.notes: Body-prefix raises specificity over GHL's stylesheet. Also explicitly override background-color AND background-image so the gradient and the flat-colour fallback both get blanked.

selectors.selector: body button > *, body button[type="submit"] > *, body .ghl-btn > *, body .ghl-submit-btn > *
selectors.purpose: Some GHL buttons wrap the label in a child <span> that carries its own colour, which beats the parent button's colour. Force every descendant to inherit colour + transparent background.

GHL's form builder applies the form-level theme colour through CSS custom properties on :root. Names vary by builder version. Hijack all of them at :root with !important to re-skin the form's default theme blue at the source — much more reliable than fighting each element's `color: var(--primary)` rule downstream.
selectors.notes: Not all of these variables will exist on any given form — defining a variable that isn't referenced is a no-op, so the over-coverage is safe.

selectors.purpose: Terms-and-conditions consent block (checkbox + legal text + link). Exact class varies by GHL version.
selectors.notes: Not confirmed in either community reference. Inspect when building. The structure is typically a `<label>` containing a checkbox + text + anchor.

selectors.purpose: Anchor tags inside the form (terms-and-conditions, privacy policy, etc).
Survey question set wrapper (multi-step forms only).
Survey footer area containing the navigation buttons.
Survey 'Next' button (advances to the next step).
Survey 'Back' button (returns to the previous step).
Survey progress bar (track + fill + label text).
Post-submission confirmation screen.
The placeholder div + the actual iframe written by form_embed.js. Lives on the PARENT page, not inside the iframe. Use to size or constrain the embed externally.
selectors.notes: Placeholder id pattern: `inline-<formId>`. Iframe id is appended by the loader.

anti_patterns.why_to_avoid: Order-dependent. Breaks the moment a field is added, removed, or reordered in the form builder. The simple10 gist uses this and explicitly marks it as brittle.

anti_patterns.pattern: Targeting field types by guessing the wrapper class
anti_patterns.why_to_avoid: GHL has no per-type wrapper class — the wrapper is always `.form-field-wrapper` regardless of whether the input is text, checkbox, radio, signature, or file.
anti_patterns.use_instead: Match on the inner input's type attribute or its data-q name.

anti_patterns.why_to_avoid: GHL's own stylesheet loads after the Custom CSS panel. Without !important, almost every rule loses the cascade.
anti_patterns.use_instead: Use !important on every override rule. Standard practice for these embeds.

anti_patterns.pattern: Only targeting .ghl-btn.ghl-submit-btn for the submit button
anti_patterns.why_to_avoid: Newer-version forms (2026+) often render submit buttons without the .ghl-btn / .ghl-submit-btn classes. The button keeps GHL's default theme blue because the override never matches.
anti_patterns.use_instead: Use the broad submit-button-broad selector + the theme-variables :root override together. Belt-and-braces.

anti_patterns.why_to_avoid: GHL applies the theme colour as `background-color` and sometimes adds a `background-image` gradient. The shorthand `background` should reset both, but inline `background-image` styles can survive — set them explicitly to `none`.
anti_patterns.use_instead: Set all three: background, background-color, AND background-image.
